task_context.description: This is a systematic electromagnetic calorimetry study investigating the relationship between detector depth and shower containment performance. The task involves simulating electromagnetic showers in a homogeneous lead tungstate (PbWO4) calorimeter at three different depths (16, 20, and 25 radiation lengths) across multiple electron beam energies (1, 5, and 20 GeV). The study requires measuring energy containment fractions, energy resolution, and shower maximum positions to characterize how calorimeter thickness affects electromagnetic shower development and energy leakage. This is a parametric design study that combines detector physics simulation with performance optimization to establish minimum detector thickness requirements for acceptable energy containment.
task_context.goal: Determine the minimum calorimeter depth required to keep energy leakage below 1% at 20 GeV electron energy
constraints: Energy leakage must be below 1% at 20 GeV
Use homogeneous PbWO4 calorimeter block only
Run one depth configuration per geometry call
Use tractable simulation parameters for computational efficiency
